title: Statistical-integrity checklist (definitions, flags, periods, uncertainty)
context: FAOSTAT indicators carry technical definitions, multi-year moving averages (e.g. 3-year PoU averages with lower/upper uncertainty bounds), and status flags (official/estimated/imputed/aggregate) that are easy to misread or strip out. A committed, reviewable statistical-integrity checklist is needed so that every figure in every brief can be verified to resolve to a derivative-dataset row, flags are preserved, uncertainty is shown, and chronic/structural indicators are never conflated with acute-crisis concepts.
objective: Create the statistical-integrity checklist that every brief must pass, covering FAOSTAT flag handling, reference periods, uncertainty preservation, and the chronic-vs-acute distinction.
acceptanceCriteria: Enumerates how to handle each FAOSTAT status flag (official, estimated, imputed, aggregate) so estimated or imputed values are never presented as observed in a brief.
Specifies how to handle 3-year moving averages (e.g. PoU), provisional vs. final figures, and imputed values, including required disclosure wording.
Requires every figure in a brief to resolve to a specific row in the committed derivative dataset; un-sourced numbers fail the checklist.
Requires published uncertainty (e.g. PoU lower/upper bounds) to be carried into the brief and never collapsed to a point estimate without disclosure.
Distinguishes chronic/structural food-security indicators (in scope) from acute-crisis/famine concepts (out of scope, escalate/exclude), so the two are never conflated.
Produces a committed, per-brief pass/flag checklist artifact that a domain-expert reviewer can sign off; one unresolved flag blocks publication.
Output licensed CC-BY-4.0; commit is DCO signed-off.
resources: PLAN.md — Solution approach (pipeline step 4: Statistical QA)
PLAN.md — Quality, review & risk gates section
FAOSTAT Suite of Food Security Indicators documentation
output: A committed statistical-integrity checklist document and per-brief checklist template, ready for use by every brief-authoring task.
